◈ CHROMACAL
WHOLE-HOME HOLIDAY LIGHTING
CONNECT TO HOME ASSISTANT
ChromaCal runs inside HA and talks directly to your lights. Enter your HA URL and a long-lived access token.
Generate at: HA → Your Profile → Security → Long-Lived Access Tokens
PICK YOUR LOOK
Choose a theme — applies instantly so you can see it right now. Change it anytime later in Settings.
YOUR REGION
Sets your primary holiday calendar. You can blend in other regions later in Settings.
🇺🇸
United States
US federal + cultural
🇨🇦
Canada
CA federal + Remembrance Day
🇬🇧
United Kingdom
Bank holidays + UK customs
🇦🇺
Australia / NZ
AU national + ANZAC Day
🇪🇺
Europe
EU-wide + major national days
🌏
Asia-Pacific
JP, KR, CN, SG, NZ
🌎
Latin America
MX, BR, PR + more
🌍
Global / Other
Universal + floating observances
WHAT MATTERS TO YOUR HOME
Pick what you want ChromaCal to celebrate. Nothing runs until you choose it. Change anytime in Settings.
ADD YOUR FIRST LIGHT
Works with any light — porch, living room, bedroom. You can add more later and group them by zone.
💡 Not sure what entity ID to enter? Help & troubleshooting ▾
🔷 Zigbee (ZHA) — create a group first for best results
  • Go to Settings → Devices & Services → ZHA → Configure
  • Select Manage Zigbee Groups → Create Group
  • Name it (e.g. "Front Porch"), add your bulbs
  • HA creates a single entity like light.front_porch — use that here
  • Zigbee groups send one network command to all bulbs at once — faster and more synchronized than individual commands
💡 You can also use individual bulb entity IDs directly. ChromaCal sends to each one — Zigbee groups are just more efficient and instant.
🟢 Z-Wave / Matter / WiFi lights
  • Find your entity in Settings → Devices & Services → Entities
  • Use the entity ID shown (e.g. light.living_room)
  • For multiple bulbs: create a Light Group under Settings → Devices → Helpers → Light Group
🔧 Common issues
Light doesn't respond
Verify entity ID in HA → Developer Tools → States. Check it exists and is a light.* entity, not switch.*.
Color looks wrong or washed out
Try Bold lighting style. Some bulbs default to color temperature mode — setting a color should override it.
Zigbee group not syncing
All bulbs must be in ZHA. Restart HA after adding to a group. Check ZHA logs for errors.
Switch entity won't change color
A switch.* entity can only toggle on/off. You need a light.* entity from a smart color bulb.
USB 3.0 interference
Move your Zigbee USB stick to a USB 2.0 port or use a USB extension cable to get it away from USB 3.0.
Lights unreachable at range
Add more plug-in Zigbee devices (smart plugs) — they act as repeaters and extend mesh coverage.
🎉
CHROMACAL IS READY
Your home is set up and ready to celebrate. Next, install the automation files in Home Assistant so ChromaCal can run on schedule automatically.
📦 Download automation YAML files from GitHub 📖 Read the installation guide
◈ CHROMACAL
The whole world celebrates with light.
Now you can too.

▸ Quick Controls

▸ House View
Already have a 3D model or floor plan from floor3d-card, Sweet Home 3D, or just a photo of your house? Drop the file in the same /www/ folder as ChromaCal and point to it below. Click anywhere on it to place a marker, then assign it to one of your lights — markers update live.
No file loaded yet.
2D: any image — .png .jpg .svg
3D: .glb or .gltf — exports from Blender, Sweet Home 3D, or floor3d-card

Enter a path above (relative to your HA /www/ folder) and click Load.
💡 Click anywhere on the image/model above to drop a marker, then pick which light it represents below. Markers glow with that light's live color.
Honor your heritage. Light your home.  ·  ChromaCal v1.0.0  ·  GitHub  · 
ADD / EDIT LIGHT
Click ⟳ Detect to auto-populate from your HA instance
Your "Sunset Fade In" automation takes ~45 min (sunset-30min → full bright). Setting +45 min means ChromaCal waits until warm-up finishes before switching colors.
When does your existing warm-up automation start? ChromaCal shows the fade-in zone starting here so the timeline matches what's actually happening to your lights.
If set, ChromaCal fires this color temperature at sunset (before the offset timer starts) so your warm-up automation brightens the lights to THIS color/temp. Leave at "Don't set" if your existing automation already sets the right warm-up color.
Security white lights the porch at 22:00 before lights-off at 23:00. Cooler = brighter looking, warmer = cozy/amber.
ADD PERSONAL EVENT
COLOR OVERRIDE
Set a custom color for a duration, then automatically resume the schedule.
